title: Restrict Deployment Access for CI/CD
description:
Implement least-privilege RBAC for a CI/CD ServiceAccount that can create and update Deployments in `staging` but cannot delete them or touch other resources.

Create a `staging` namespace, `ci-deployer` ServiceAccount, a Role granting `create`/`update`/`get` on `deployments` (apiGroup `apps`), and a RoleBinding. Confirm `can-i`: create → yes, delete → no, create pods → no.

**Common Pitfalls**: Deployments are in `apiGroups: ["apps"]` — using `[""]` means the rule silently does not apply. Use a RoleBinding, not ClusterRoleBinding, to scope access to staging only.
steps: Create a ServiceAccount named ci-deployer in the staging namespace
Create a Role named deployment-manager in staging with rules: apiGroups ["apps"], resources ["deployments"], verbs ["create", "update", "get"]
Create a RoleBinding named ci-deployer-binding in staging that binds deployment-manager to ci-deployer
Verify ci-deployer can create deployments in staging (kubectl auth can-i create deployments --as system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-deployer -n staging → yes)
Verify ci-deployer cannot delete deployments and cannot create pods directly in staging (both → no)
hints: Deployments are in apiGroups: ["apps"] — this is the most common RBAC mistake
Pods are in apiGroups: [""] — even though Deployments manage Pods, the resources are different
Use kubectl auth can-i create pods --as system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-deployer -n staging to verify pod access is denied
The RoleBinding namespace must be staging, matching the Role's namespace

editor_config.initial_code:
# Namespace
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
---
# ServiceAccount
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: ci-deployer
  namespace: staging
---
# Role: create and update deployments only
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: deployment-manager
  namespace: staging
rules:
- apiGroups: # TODO: ["apps"] — deployments are NOT in the core API group
  resources: # TODO: ["deployments"]
  verbs: # TODO: ["create", "update", "get"]
---
# RoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: ci-deployer-binding
  namespace: staging
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: ci-deployer
  namespace: staging
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: deployment-manager
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: staging
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: ci-deployer
  namespace: staging
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: Role
metadata:
  name: deployment-manager
  namespace: staging
rules:
- apiGroups: ["apps"]
  resources: ["deployments"]
  verbs: ["create", "update", "get"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: RoleBinding
metadata:
  name: ci-deployer-binding
  namespace: staging
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: ci-deployer
  namespace: staging
roleRef:
  kind: Role
  name: deployment-manager
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
echo "✓ Resources applied"

# Namespace
kubectl get namespace staging > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ Namespace staging exists" || { echo "✗ Namespace staging not found"; exit 1; }

# ServiceAccount
kubectl get serviceaccount ci-deployer -n staging > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ServiceAccount ci-deployer exists" || { echo "✗ ci-deployer not found in staging"; exit 1; }

# Role
kubectl get role deployment-manager -n staging > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ Role deployment-manager exists" || { echo "✗ Role deployment-manager not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify apiGroups includes apps
apigroups=$(kubectl get role deployment-manager -n staging -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].apiGroups[*]}')
if echo "$apigroups" | grep -q "apps"; then
  echo "✓ Role uses apiGroups apps"
else
  echo "✗ Role apiGroups should include 'apps'. Found: $apigroups"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify resources
resources=$(kubectl get role deployment-manager -n staging -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].resources[*]}')
if echo "$resources" | grep -q "deployments"; then
  echo "✓ Role targets deployments"
else
  echo "✗ Role resources should include deployments. Found: $resources"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify verbs
verbs=$(kubectl get role deployment-manager -n staging -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].verbs[*]}')
if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "create" && echo "$verbs" | grep -q "update"; then
  echo "✓ Role includes create and update verbs"
else
  echo "✗ Role verbs should include create and update. Found: $verbs"
  exit 1
fi

if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "delete"; then
  echo "✗ Role should NOT include delete verb"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Role does NOT include delete verb"

# RoleBinding
kubectl get rolebinding ci-deployer-binding -n staging > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ RoleBinding ci-deployer-binding exists" || { echo "✗ RoleBinding ci-deployer-binding not found"; exit 1; }

# Test can create deployments
can_create=$(kubectl auth can-i create deployments --as system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-deployer -n staging)
if [ "$can_create" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "✓ ci-deployer CAN create deployments in staging"
else
  echo "✗ ci-deployer cannot create deployments in staging (got: $can_create)"
  exit 1
fi

# Test cannot delete deployments
can_delete=$(kubectl auth can-i delete deployments --as system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-deployer -n staging)
if [ "$can_delete" = "no" ]; then
  echo "✓ ci-deployer CANNOT delete deployments (correct)"
else
  echo "✗ ci-deployer should NOT be able to delete deployments (got: $can_delete)"
  exit 1
fi

# Test cannot create pods directly
can_pod=$(kubectl auth can-i create pods --as system:serviceaccount:staging:ci-deployer -n staging)
if [ "$can_pod" = "no" ]; then
  echo "✓ ci-deployer CANNOT create pods directly (correct)"
else
  echo "✗ ci-deployer should NOT be able to create pods directly (got: $can_pod)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
